name: Incident Response Playbook
description: Execute a structured incident response workflow from detection through triage, mitigation, communication, and post-mortem.

inputs.name: incident_signal
inputs.description: Alert payload, error logs, or user report describing the incident.

inputs.name: affected_services
inputs.description: List of services, endpoints, or infrastructure components impacted.

inputs.name: severity_classification
inputs.description: Pre-assessed severity level (SEV1-SEV4) or criteria for classification.

inputs.name: communication_channels
inputs.description: Stakeholder notification targets and status page integration.

outputs.name: triage_summary
outputs.description: Incident classification, blast radius, and initial severity assessment.

outputs.name: mitigation_actions
outputs.description: Immediate containment steps taken to reduce impact.

outputs.name: communication_log
outputs.description: Timestamped stakeholder updates and status page entries.

outputs.name: post_mortem_draft
outputs.description: Timeline, root cause hypothesis, impact assessment, and action items.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is active incident response, not proactive monitoring setup or capacity planning.
Classify severity and confirm affected services before executing any mitigation action.
Verify each mitigation action reduced impact metrics before proceeding.
Return triage summary, mitigation log, communication record, and post-mortem draft.

steps.description: Analyze incident signal, classify severity, and determine blast radius across affected services.
Page on-call engineers, assign incident commander, and open a war room or bridge.
Apply immediate containment actions such as rollbacks, traffic shifts, or circuit breakers.
Send stakeholder updates via configured channels and update the status page.
Analyze logs, metrics, and recent changes to identify the root cause hypothesis.
Compile incident timeline, impact assessment, root cause, and corrective action items.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read runbooks, incident templates, and service dependency maps.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write triage notes, communication logs, and post-mortem documents.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.purpose: Execute rollback commands, health checks, and diagnostic queries.

verification.method: Incident has a documented severity level and blast radius within 10 minutes of detection.
Key impact metrics show improvement within 30 minutes of mitigation.
Communication log shows updates sent at defined intervals.
Post-mortem draft includes timeline, root cause, impact, and at least 3 action items.

success_criteria: Incident is classified and response team assembled within SLA.
Stakeholders receive timely updates throughout the incident.
Post-mortem is drafted within 24 hours with actionable items.

rollback.when: If a mitigation action worsens the incident, reverse it and escalate to the next containment strategy.
